results.title: Senior Manager Enterprise Data, BI & AI (Pulte Mortgage) – Augustana College
highlights:
Senior Manager Enterprise Data, BI & AI (Pulte Mortgage) – Augustana College
...
# Senior Manager Enterprise Data, BI & AI (Pulte Mortgage)
...
At Pulte Mortgage, we’re more than a lender-we’re a team driven by purpose. Since 1972, we’ve helped over 700,000 families finance their dream homes through innovative lending solutions and a commitment to doing the right thing. As a wholly owned subsidiary of PulteGroup, we offer a people-first culture rooted in collaboration, integrity, and daily positivity. Join us in Denver, CO, and be part of a company recognized by Fortune and Great Place to Work for building meaningful careers, supporting community impact, and creating a workplace where you can thrive.
...
Job SummaryThe Senior Manager, Enterprise Data, BI & AI (EDBI), is a strategic people leader responsible for advancing enterprise data and analytics capabilities through the leadership and development of high-performing teams. This role oversees the implementation of scalable data, reporting, and governance solutions while driving team performance, delivery execution, and the realization of business value from data products.As the primary owner of EDBI priorities and backlog, the Senior Manager defines the “what” and “why” of data and analytics initiatives, guiding the team on priorities and ensuring alignment to enterprise strategy. This role operates at the intersection of people leadership, strategy, and product, translating complex business needs into actionable features, datasets, and reporting solutions that maximize the value of the organization’s data platform.The Senior Manager partners closely with business leaders, architects, and engineering teams, serving as a trusted stakeholder advisor to ensure solutions are aligned, governed, scalable, and impactful. Success in this role requires strong leadership presence, the ability to influence across functions, and a focus on developing team capability while delivering high-quality outcomes within Microsoft Fabric.
...
- Drive delivery of data, reporting, and governance solutions, ensuring quality, scalability, and consistency across the organization
- Partner with business leaders and IT to understand priorities, translate needs into actionable solutions, and drive adoption of data and analytics capabilities
- Oversee the development and management of data solutions, including data pipelines, models, dashboards, and self-service analytics tools
...
- Establish and maintain enterprise standards for data quality, KPI definition, and reporting governance
...
- Lead and develop a high-performing Enterprise Data, BI & AI (EDBI) team, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement
- Set clear priorities for the team, aligning work to enterprise goals and ensuring focus on initiatives that deliver the greatest business impact
- Provide leadership and guidance on complex data and analytics initiatives, supporting informed decision-making across the business
...
- Minimum high school diploma or equivalent (GED) required.
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related discipline preferred
- 6 -8 years of data, analytics, or technology experience
- 3+ years of leadership experience
- Experience in agile delivery and backlog management
- Strong business-to-technical translation skills
- Proven proficiency in leadership, HR management, strategic planning, vendor management, cybersecurity, communication, problem solving, compliance and risk management.
...
- Deep understanding of business practices and operations.
...
- Exceptional analytical skills.
...
- Strong written and verbal communication skills.
...
- Proficiency with MS Office / MS Office 365 tools including Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook, OneNote, SharePoint.
...
- Microsoft Fabric or Azure experience
- Self-service analytics experience
- Regulated industry experience
- CSPO or similar certification

results.title: PulteGroup AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
PulteGroup is emerging as a pioneer in AI and construction technology among American homebuilders, with notable initiatives spanning robotic construction, smart neighborhoods, and innovation laboratories[1][2]. The company's most significant AI initiative is a partnership with Australian robotics firm FBR to test the Hadrian X® system, an AI-guided construction robot capable of building structural walls in a single day[1][3]. This pilot, conducted at PulteGroup's TerraWalk community in Babcock Ranch, Florida, represents one of only 10 homes in the United States constructed using this advanced system[1]. The company has also established an 'Innovation Way living laboratory' at Babcock Ranch to test emerging technologies including smart home automation and sustainable building practices[1][2]. Additionally, PulteGroup has partnered with Georgia Power to develop Atlanta's first Smart Neighborhood™, featuring technology-enhanced homes with rooftop solar, battery storage, and home automation systems[2].

### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/brandonhart_ai-adoption-for-the-sake-of-ai-adoption-is-activity-7461396756413616128-Kq6g
...
AI adoption for the sake of AI adoption is a trap.
...
​If a legitimate AI usecase exists within a key business activity, apply it. If it doesn't? Walk away.  ​That is the foundation of a winning AI strategy.
...
But execution requires a specific kind of leadership:  ​If you are an AI leader, you cannot just be a tech leader. You must know the business inside and out.
...
​You can't go to business line leaders and ask, "Where do you want AI?" Their focus is naturally siloed. To be successful, you have to be the business expert who identifies where AI makes sense.
...
​AI alignment is not a democratic process. True success with AI requires top-down business expertise, clear guardrails, and a relentless prioritization of value over hype. You adopted AI.

# Data Scientist (GenAI and Machine Learning)
...

- Job Type: Remote
...
Job Category: Data AI & ML Jobs
...
### Responsibilities
...
- Develop and apply machine learning models, including techniques such as decision trees and forecasting, to solve business problems
- Use generative artificial intelligence methods to extract insights from complex document sets, including structured and unstructured text
- Perform data analysis, feature engineering, and model development using Python
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to interpret data, communicate findings, and support decision-making
- Translate domain-specific language and requirements into actionable data science solutions
- Continuously refine models and analytical approaches to improve accuracy and performance
...
### Required experience and skills
...
- Strong background in data science, with hands-on experience in machine learning model development
- Proficiency in Python, including libraries such as Pandas for data manipulation and analysis
- Working knowledge of SQL for querying and managing data
- Experience with generative artificial intelligence, including prompt-based techniques
- Solid understanding of statistical methods and analytical techniques
- Experience with machine learning approaches such as tree-based models and forecasting methods
- Ability to communicate complex analytical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

# Data Scientist + ML Engineer (Gen AI)
...
- Data Scientist + ML Engineer (Gen AI)
...
- We are looking for a highly skilled Data Scientist + ML Engineer (Generative AI) to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for developing, fine-tuning, and applying advanced generative AI models - including diffusion models, large language models (LLMs), and other state-of-the-art architectures. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional partners in research, data engineering, and operations to deliver high-quality machine learning solutions and scalable datasets.
...
- Design and Implement LLM-Driven Synthetic Data Pipelines: Design and build workflows using LLMs and Gen AI techniques to create high-volume, high-quality synthetic data for model training and testing.
...

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Our company is hiring a Founding AI / ML Engineer to help architect and ship the next generation of AI-powered workflows for construction. This is not a research seat or a demo-builder role. The company wants product- minded engineers who build reliable, production-grade AI systems that solve real customer problems in lean teams. You will work across multimodal AI, document intelligence, agentic workflows, and evaluation systems, turning cutting-edge capabilities into software estimators that depend on daily. You will report to the Chief AI Officer (eventually CTO) and work directly alongside the founders and customers.

## Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health optimization on a global scale. Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together. Primary Responsibility: Comply with the terms and conditions of the employment contract, company policies and procedures, and any and all directives (such as, but not limited to, transfer and/or re-assignment to different work locations, change in teams and/or work shifts, policies in regards to flexibility of work benefits and/or work environment, alternative work arrangements, and other decisions that may arise due to the changing business environment). The Company may adopt, vary or rescind these policies and directives in its absolute discretion and without any limitation (implied or otherwise) on its ability to do so Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or higher in computer science engineering. Overall 5+ years of experience in AI ML roles Experience in implementing real projects using Gen AI, Fine tuning LLMs , RAG. Experience in implementing enterprise level Agentic solution using any of the open frameworks (Langgraph, Google ADK, CrewAI, etc.) Experience with vector databases / embeddings. Experience in Document AI / OCR / extraction solutions and structured output creation Exposure implementing multiple machine learning and AI techniques such as supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning, Deep learning, NLP Familiarity with NLP literature, thrust areas, conference venues, and code repositories Familiarity with UI tools like Streamlit, flask etc , FAST APIs , Rest APIs , Docker containers Proficiency in Python, pytorch , SQL and other programming languages for data analysis and modeling with atleast 5+ years of experience. Proficiency in libraries such as OpenAI API Proficiency with cloud development environments such as Azure, AWS, and GCP Understanding of Both Opensource and Open AI LLMs and RAG architecture Proven good grasp of using LLMs, Deploying LLMs Proven excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, including the ability to disaggregate issues, identify root causes and recommend solutions At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...

Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.

- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.

, and fairness.

...

...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.

- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...

...

results.title: Johnson Controls – Associate Software Engineer (Data Scientist) - Job Society
highlights:
Johnson Controls is seeking a talented and passionate Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist to join its Data Science & AI team in Pune. This role is an excellent opportunity for aspiring data science professionals who want to work on advanced machine learning, artificial intelligence, and cloud-based analytics solutions. As part of a global technology-driven organization, you will contribute to building intelligent systems that transform complex business challenges into data-driven solutions. The position offers exposure to cutting-edge technologies including machine learning, deep learning, generative AI, cloud computing, and MLOps, making it an ideal opportunity for professionals looking to accelerate their careers in the rapidly evolving field of data science.
...
In this role, you will collaborate with data engineers, AI specialists, cloud architects, and business stakeholders to develop scalable machine learning models and AI-powered applications. You will work extensively with Python, SQL, Microsoft Azure, Azure Machine Learning, Azure Databricks, and Azure OpenAI services to create intelligent solutions that generate measurable business value. The position involves the complete machine learning lifecycle, including data preparation, feature engineering, model development, deployment, monitoring, and optimization. The Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist role provides an exceptional platform for learning and innovation. Johnson Controls encourages continuous skill development through mentorship programs, certification sponsorships, and hands-on exposure to enterprise-scale AI and machine learning projects. You will gain experience working with modern technologies such as Generative AI, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), deep learning frameworks, and cloud-based MLOps solutions. This position is ideal for candidates who are eager to solve real-world business problems using data, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics while contributing to transformative technology initiatives within a globally recognized organization.

The expected salary for an Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist at Johnson Controls in Pune typically ranges between ₹8 LPA and ₹16 LPA, depending on educational background, technical expertise, project experience, cloud certifications, and machine learning proficiency. Candidates with strong Azure cloud knowledge, hands-on AI and machine learning experience, deep learning expertise, and exposure to Generative AI technologies may receive compensation toward the higher end of the range, along with performance incentives, certification sponsorships, learning benefits, and long-term career growth opportunities.
